what_it_does: Stores YAML frontmatter metadata for an image attachment that targets a design-source pitch image.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, with no body content after the closing ---
target uses an image:// URI pointing to design-source/recent-pitches/p-12
added timestamp is recorded in ISO 8601 with timezone
standouts: It is a metadata stub linking an attachment id to an image asset in the design-source channel
The target is a URI-style reference rather than a filesystem path
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with a fixed set of fields: id, channel, attachment_type, target, added
References assets through a channel-scoped URI scheme
Keeps the file body empty after frontmatter
concerns: The image:// target gives no filename, extension, or resolution details
No caption, alt text, or uploader is recorded
There is no checksum or media-format field
The pitch p-12 and the actual image content are not described here
notes: A minimal, timestamped attachment record that points an image asset to a design-source pitch.
questions: What image format and dimensions does the target have?
Who uploaded or added this attachment?
What is pitch p-12 and how does this image relate to it?
Does the image:// URI resolve to an existing file?
purpose_vs_actual: The file acts as an attachment metadata stub, which matches its apparent purpose of recording an image attachment for a channel pitch.
intention_signals: It appears intended to register an image asset as an attachment to a specific design pitch in the design-source channel.
novelty: The target uses an image:// URI scheme instead of a conventional relative file path.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, typed link between a channel-memory attachment identifier and an image asset target, enabling asset tracking without embedding the asset itself.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-only attachment metadata file named att-2fa383a2.md that records an image attachment of type images targeting image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-12 with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to catalog an image asset attached to a design-source pitch for later retrieval and channel-memory indexing.
